title: Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Veterans - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Veterans 25 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Domestic Violence Issues Homeless Families Homeless Veterans Homeless Youth Human Trafficking Issues Pregnant Individuals Sexual Assault Issues Veterans ACCESS SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program with assistance through case management. Support may include move-in costs, rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, and legal aid around housing barriers. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. May require clients to be alcohol-and drug-free. Assistance depends on the availability of funding and eligibility. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: -Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness -Veteran must be present in household -Any discharge other than dishonorable Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (458) 488-1340 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ACCESS JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Access point for being placed on Jackson County's Centralized Interest List for housing. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Households experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Access also focuses specifically on serving veterans experiencing homelessness. Hours: Monday-Thursday 7:30am-6pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 779-6691 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLUE MOUNTAIN ACTION COUNCIL SSVF SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless within 30 days or less. Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in/rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 312 N 2nd Avenue Walla Walla, WA 99362 Eligibility: Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness with any discharge other than "Dishonorable." Veteran must be present in household. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-4pm Email (509) 529-4980 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Eligible veterans and their families can be supported in obtaining housing or avoiding eviction in their current housing. Case management support may include move-in/rental assistance, eviction prevention (rental and utility arrears), benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. All temporary financial assistance is subject to the Veteran's eligibility according to SSVF/VA program policies. Financial assistance depends on availability of funds. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than "Dishonorable." Veteran must be present in household and if housed on the lease. Members of a household are determined by the Veteran. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CLACKAMAS COUNTY COORDINATED HOUSING ACCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a centralized access point for households experiencing homelessness or a housing crisis through a collaboration of multiple agencies. Eligible households are reviewed for services and added to the Housing By Name List for all qualifying programs. Assistance with rent or moving costs is limited, not guaranteed, and may depend on factors such as urban growth boundaries, eviction notices, court dates, and funding. Eligibility: Individuals and families who are homeless or at imminent risk of becoming homeless seeking prevention/diversion services to stay in their current homes Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Off-peak call times: Monday-Thursday 4pm-6pm Email (503) 655-8575 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY ACTION VETERAN HOUSING ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance to veterans and their families to achieve housing stability. 364 9th Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Head of household or spouse served in the military. Household income not exceeding 50% of area median income. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-3:30pm Email (503) 325-1400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for veterans and their families.Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in/rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. May require clients to be alcohol- and drug-free. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Veterans and veteran families who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness and have an income at or below 50% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than "Dishonorable." Veteran must be present in household. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 276-1926 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers short-term housing assistance. Support may include help with finding housing, move-in or rental assistance, eviction prevention, and access to benefits, employment, education, legal services related to housing barriers, and other expenses that support stable housing. Case management is available, pending eligibility. Participation requires working with a caseworker, and some programs may require individuals to be drug- and alcohol-free. All assistance is based on eligibility and the availability of funding. 125 N 17th Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income with any discharge other than Dishonorable or General Court Martial. Veteran must be present in the household. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-5pm Email (503) 397-3511 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY BEAVERTON MULTI-SERVICE CENTER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Veteran must be present in household to participate. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 304-7140 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HARNEY COUNTY HARNEY HUB SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Veteran must be present in household. Hours: Appointment hours: Monday-Wednesday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Walk in hours: Thursday 9am-noon, first come, first served Email (541) 573-1342 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL THE GLORIA CENTER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program. Assistance may include help with move-in fees, rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. 2505 W 7th Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Veteran must be present in the household. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (541) 298-5131 200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHEAST OREGON HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers low-income and subsidized apartments. Available programs: Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers, Veterans Supportive Housing Vouchers, Section 8 Housing Voucher Homeownership, Mental Health Referred Housing, Family Self-Sufficiency, HUD Multi-Family Housing, Tax Credit Housing, and Rural Development Housing. 2608 May Lane La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Email (541) 963-5360 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPERATION REBUILD HOPE OPERATION REBUILD HOPE SERVICE NAVIGATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides service navigation to connect veterans to support groups, housing resources, and apply for benefits. 2055 Union Avenue Suite 2 North Bend, OR 97459 Eligibility: Must be Veterans Affairs Grant Per Diem (VA-GPD) eligible and pass a criminal background check Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-3pm Email (541) 808-9025 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION COOS SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers housing-focused case management, referrals to community services, limited short-term rental or deposit assistance, and housing placement support. All assistance is time-limited and based on program guidelines. 1855 Thomas Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Veteran households may be considered for SSVF if they: -Include at least one eligible veteran (per VA guidelines) -Are homeless or at risk of homelessness -Have a household income at or below 80% of Area Median Income (AMI) for Coos County -Meet additional SSVF and funding requirements Meeting eligibility criteria does not guarantee financial assistance. Hours: English, Spanish interpretation by arrangement Email (541) 435-7080 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION CURRY SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers housing-focused case management, referrals to community services, limited short-term rental or deposit assistance, and housing placement support. All assistance is time-limited and based on program guidelines. 937 Chetco Avenue Suite D Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Veteran households may be considered for SSVF if they: -Include at least one eligible veteran (per VA guidelines) -Are homeless or at risk of homelessness -Have a household income at or below 80% of Area Median Income (AMI) for Coos or Curry County -Meet additional SSVF and funding requirements Meeting eligibility criteria does not guarantee financial assistance. Hours: English, Spanish interpretation by arrangement Email (541) 469-3155 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS VETERAN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. Veterans and their families can be assisted with housing through case management, pending eligibility. If eligible, assistance and support may include move-in costs and rental assistance, eviction prevention, benefits support, employment and education services, legal aid around housing barriers, and other expenses that may support stable housing. Must be willing to work with a caseworker. Assistance in any of these programs depends on availability of funds. 7916 SE Foster Road Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans and their households, who are homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness, and have an income at or below 80% of Area Median Income. Households are only eligible if the veteran applies and is eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Walk in: Monday-Friday 10am-2pm Email (855) 425-5544 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK GARDEN VALLEY SERVICE CENTER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Supportive Services for Veteran Families (SSVF) short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. May be able to assist with rent, deposits, utilities, job search costs, child care costs, or clothing or tools needed for work. 251 NE Garden Valley Boulevard Suite 111 Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: -Veterans households at or below 60% State Median Income -Lack housing or are at the risk of losing it -Household only needs short term help to get into stable housing Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (800) 301-8226 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK JOSEPHINE COUNTY SERVICE CENTER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Supportive Services for Veteran Families (SSVF) short-term housing intervention program for low-income veterans and their families experiencing homelessness or at imminent risk of becoming homeless. May be able to assist with rent, deposits, utilities, job search costs, child care costs, or clothing or tools needed for work. 900 SE 8th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: -Veterans households at or below 60% State Median Income -Lack housing or are at the risk of losing it -Household only needs short term help to get into stable housing Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 956-4057 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK VETERAN REINTEGRATION CENTERS VETERAN REINTEGRATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a wide range of psychosocial services and counseling to support a successful transition from military to civilian life. Services provided include individual and group counseling for veterans, service members, and their families; family counseling for military-related issues; bereavement counseling for families who experience an active duty death; military sexual trauma counseling and referral; and outreach and education through programs like the Post-Deployment Health Reassessment Program. They also provide substance abuse assessment and referral, employment assessment and referral, explanations and referrals for Veterans Benefits Administration benefits, and screening and referrals for medical issues such as Traumatic Brain Injury and depression. Website Eligibility: Veterans and their family members Hours: Varies by location (877) 927-8387 877-- 247 Visit Website More Details VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to veterans in accessing VA and community services. Services may include short-term case management, onsite primary care, access to medical, mental health, and substance abuse treatment, and access to VA housing programs, including Grant and Per Diem program, HUD/VASH, Veterans Recovery House and Supportive Service for Veteran Families (SSVF) if found eligible. These grants do not provide immediate assistance, are sometimes one-time, and may include case management. Help with finding market rate or subsidized housing or linkage to community housing programs. Assistance with expediting VA Benefit claims and access to employment programs such as Supported Employment, Veterans Reintegration program, and State employment. Access to computers, clothing, and hygiene items is available onsite. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Veterans must be present in the household and must be the one to request services. Some services available for Veterans who are not eligible for full VA Healthcare benefits. Hours: -Business hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 7:30am-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays -Walk-in hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-3pm, Thursday 10am-3pm. No walk-ins after 2:30pm, better to go earlier in the day. Closed federal holidays.
